# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Envoy configuration with HTTP filters for request processing. Define filter chains including router filter for traffic routing. Set up retry policies and timeout configurations for backend connections. This is for our service mesh data plane.
# Category: envoy_authz_filter_missing

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata: 
 name : envoy-cfg   #envly configuration file should be named as 'envoy' if it isn’t already used by another resource or in use case scenario for Envoy, we may need a different approach. However the above YAML code does not support this feature because ConfigMaps are immutable and do not natively handle custom configurations like HTTP Filters
data:  # Defining Data here with keys-values pair which has to match envoys configuration file structure or else it will throw an error while updating/reading config. Envoy uses YAML format for its Configuration Files, so that's what we use in this code snippet as well (Note data key is just a placeholder and needs replacement)
  xds_replica: "<your-xDS>" # Replace with your service name which has to be consistent across all clusters defined under 'clusters'. e.g., myservice, productpage etc... This also should match in other config files as well if different environments/services are being utilized
  xds_resource: "<your-xDS>" # Same above but refers resources like RouterGateways or Endpoints for the service mesh to interact with. e.g., myservice, productpage etc... The name should also be consistent across all clusters defined under 'clusters'.
envoyFilterChainDefinitions:  // HTTP Filter Chain definitions here will contain various chain types (Router) and associated filters configurations for request processing via Envoy Proxy. Including Routing, TLS Termination etc...
